metadata.title:
WANT SIMPKINS TO DEFY


GOV, C0OD|NC

pages.raw_text:
A move has been started among
miners to cause Jack Simpkins,
the hunted member of the execu-
tive board of the Western Fedora
ton of Miners, to once more go
within the state of Idaho and chal-
lenge Gov. Gooding to arrest and
prosecute him for alleged implica-
ton in the murder of ex-Gov.
Steunenberg, for which crime Har-
ry Orchard is now in Jail and for
which Meyer and Pettibone, other
members of the executive board,
were tried and found mot guilty.


The plan is now to test the for
mer statement that the Idaho au-
thorities are anxious to apprehend
Simpkins and find if Gooding will
be willing to put the state further
in debt in order TO obtain the testi-
mony which it was claimed would
be secured against the miners if
Simpkins could be located. The
latter has been in Spokane several
times while the alleged search for
him was on. 1t Is figured that
Simpkins, in the light Of claims
made regarding him by the prose-
cution, would now be an embar-
RAISING proposition for the gov
ernor TO face.


Enmity existing between Capt
Swain of the Thiele agency and
Gooding is also a factor in the
case. Following the Steunenberg
murder the Thiele agency did the
work of capturing Harry Orchard
and fixing the crime upon him.
Then the Pinkertons and Detective
MCFARLAND got into the case and
were engaged by the state, through
Gov. Gooding's influence.


Both Simpkins and Capt. Swain
are now said to be in Butte. Mont.
